item.title: “Ultimate Dog Tease” pooch has passed away:(
item.description: I got so many laughs from that video. Clark the dog’s family recently announced that sadly, he is no longer with us. Raise your glass for “The maple kind.” He was a good boy and he will be sorely missed:( Here is the video that made him famous https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nGeKSiCQkPw

item.dc:creator: svelorum

item.title: Wholesome Saturday DUMP of all around good feels (no politics)
item.description:
1. Didn't expect that cute face
2. More societies should decide to be like this
3. Imagine getting this cake as a kid
4. Recreation of the Sumerian city of Uruk, considered first civilized city in the world 6500-4000 BC.
5. I just realized I'm watching this while sitting in the same position. It IS comfy!
6. Love at first site
7. We need that purchase from time to time
8. This dude visited some locations from LOTR films. Here are the exact coordinates if you end up in New Zealand one day
9. How to DAD. A tutorial.
10. Dog "sharing" some food. Middle boy didn't get the memo
11. Not a bad place to live
12. That is one pretty smile
13. Dogs are always super duper happy to see their owner
14. When you let your teenage son write the script for your job marketing video (NGL made me smile : )
15. Thank you if you made it all the way to the bottom. Finally I would like to share something that's a passion of mine and lights up my day when I get to do it. I've been working very hard (in the past 18 months) on a humorous Stanley Parable-like game with a touch of GladOS from Portal. It's called "Do NOT Press The Button". If you like those kinds of games and wish to support small indie dev teams please wishlist the game : https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Thank you, and if this dump made you smile at least once, I am happy with it! : )
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: I can’t even go see them
item.description:
I’m talking with the foster coordinator, Amy, about my foster kittens. They were all diagnosed with panleukopenia this morning, which is a very contagious, very fatal disease. They have all been quarantined in the shelter and started on four different types of medication. I naively thought I could pop in and see them every day. I should have realized that the shelter would be taking every kind of precaution to prevent the spread of the disease.
But I am heartbroken. I love these babies, I named these babies. I watched them grow from teeny little creatures to kittens who use the litter box and eat dry food and rush the door when I come in and demand pets and snuggles. And I can’t be there for them. I’ve got to rely on the shelter staff to send me updates. I feel so useless.
Taquito, Chimichanga, Queso, Frijoles, Flan, Crunchwrap Supreme, Nacho and yes - even Gordita - are part of my family. I have to say, I SO appreciate all the love Imgur has shown me and my babies. What an amazing community I am apart of. So many of you are invested in me and my kittens’ story. I promise to keep you informed of every picture, video, and update that I get. My lap feels empty but my heart feels full. Thank you, Imgur.
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: I don’t even know what’s in this dump
item.description:
here we go
never dumped before
emptying the archives. back into the sea
you might notice some themes
i never get to play anymore.
“concepts of a plague”
this one isn’t funny, sorry team
not my dog. but he loves the snow too
maybe more to come? i guess
item.dc:creator: bestbanting

item.title: Misogyny
item.description: ETA: please vote the whole ballot, up and down. Vote every primary. Don’t worry about “I didn’t research the commissioner of agriculture and don’t feel qualified”. Omg—as progressives we worry about that. Go grab a blue voter guide. If that isn’t available, grab a red voter guide and select anyone not on the red guide. I just had this talk with a friend yesterday. We have a commissioner of insurance known as “rate hike Mike” and he approves every insurance hike the insurance companies suggest. If you’re urban, you might think commissioner of agriculture might have nothing to do with you, but in my state that role is responsible for making sure scales at grocery stores are accurate—that affects everyone. Vote vote vote.
item.dc:creator: Wirefish

item.title: Rules for thee not for we.
item.dc:creator: pixelpatron

item.title: Walking with a cane (my first time)
item.description: I am physically disabled. This video was filmed in 2020, a few months after my life-changing SDR surgery. This video also marked the very first time I was able to walk with a single point cane, thanks to SDR. It was a huge deal at the time and still is! Nowadays, I walk with a cane indoors and use forearm crutches outdoors. Take a look at my other video if you’re interested in seeing that! ? I originally posted this here in 2020 but lost access to that account. Under this new account, I wanted to repost this video in honor of the surgeon who performed this surgery, who recently passed away. ?️ ?? May he always be remembered in the SDR and disabled community. ❤️ thank you sir! You changed my life. EDIT: feel free to follow my popular blog, https://thespasticdiplege.blog/ , where you can also find links to my Instagram!
item.dc:creator: mcricket20

item.title: Rescued Baby Hummingbird Reunites with Mom, Forms Unlikely Bond with Rescuer
item.description: After a tiny baby hummingbird got lost and was on the brink of death, a kind human stepped in to save it. Just minutes later, the baby’s mother found her child safe and sound. Over the following days, an incredible bond formed between the mother, her baby, and the caring human who saved the young bird's life.
item.dc:creator: CsharpDeveloperWhosLoveTacosandLadies

item.dc:creator: ironman3010

item.title: 15 months weight loss progress - 70 pounds down, 50 to go...
item.description:
This is me currently, at a girls night last weekend.
This was in June 2023. I was at my peak at 274 pounds.
1 year ago at a formal event. I was so proud of my progress here already as I had already lost 25 or so pounds.
Same dress the other day. Now 204 pounds. I'm aiming to get under 160, but going slow and steady. I'll be honest, the majority of it was diet, and was kicked off by changing my ADHD meds. The appetite reduction side effect kicked in full force and I dropped 15 pounds in the first month. I stopped emotional binge eating. I was able to reflect and work on myself, my eating habits, and my activity level. Starting to exercise at 274 would have been near impossible and probably dangerous I was so out of shape. The energy boost from losing that first 15 helped me get more active, and made it seem easier than it would have been at my peak weight. Anyway, I feel so much better and I dropped from a size 24 to a 16. I buy clothes that don't have a number in front of an X. Most importantly, I can keep up with and play with my kids.
item.dc:creator: pygmypufftrampstamp
